It was an incredibly cool experience, and a very beautiful place with an unreal view of the weight of Los Angeles, everyone who comes here as a tourist advises to visit this place one of the first, the views are just amazing

You simply have to do this, at least once. It’s amazing. Fantastic views, but man it can get busy. If you’re able to check to see what else is happening in the area, especially if you’re on a timeline. The observatory itself is a great visit. Try to be there at dusk if you can to get the best views

There's plenty to see, such as Fukuoka's square, Tesla's coil. The exhibition, of course, and the volunteers who explain are very professional. I don't understand all of English because I'm short, but if you're going to LA, I think it's a must visit. Parking was difficult, but I succeeded.
